request.question: What was Robert Tree Cody's adopted father's most prominent PSA role?

title: Tachibana Dōsetsu
Robert Tree Cody
Bhuvanaikabahu VI of Kotte
Kujō Fusazane
Iron Eyes Cody
Problem Child 2
Shane Shamrock
Thadeus Nguyễn Văn Lý

Sumako Matsui
sentences: Tachibana Dōsetsu (立花 道雪 , April 22, 1513 – November 2, 1585) , born Betsugi Akitsura (戸次鑑連 ) , also as Bekki Akitsura, and Bekki Dōsetsu, was a Japanese samurai of the Sengoku period who served the Ōtomo clan.
He was the father of Tachibana Ginchiyo and adopted father of Tachibana Muneshige.
Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.
He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.
Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Bhuvanekabahu VI (Sapumal Kumaraya) (Sinhala:සපුමල් කුමාරයා, Tamil: செண்பகப் பெருமாள்) or Chempaha Perumal (died 1480) was by self admission an adopted son of Parakramabâhu VI whose principal achievement was the conquest of Jaffna Kingdom in the year 1447 or 1450.
He ruled the Kingdom for 17 years when he was apparently summoned to the south after the demise of his adopted father.

According to a primary source "Rajavaliya", he killed the grand son of Parakrama Bahu VI namely Vira Parakrama Bahu or Jaya Bahu (1468 – c. 1470) but Do Couto, however, who was well-informed, says after a few years' reign the king died and his half-witted son was put on the throne by his aunt, who two years later finding herself unable to rule sent for Sapumal Kumaraya from Jaffna.
Kujō Fusazane (九条 房実 , 1290–1327) , son of regent Tadanori and adopted son of Moronori, was a "kugyō" or Japanese court noble of the Kamakura period (1185–1333).
He held a regent position kampaku from 1323 to 1324.
A daughter of Nijō Kanemoto was one of his wives with whom he adopted Michinori, son of his adopted father Moronori.
Iron Eyes Cody (born Espera Oscar de Corti April 3, 1904 – January 4, 1999) was an Italian-American actor.
He portrayed Native Americans in Hollywood films.
He also played a Native American shedding a tear about litter in one of the country's most well-known television PSAs, "Keep America Beautiful".
In 1996, Cody's half-sister said that he was of Italian ancestry, but he denied it.

Problem Child 2 is a 1991 American comedy film and a sequel to the 1990 film "Problem Child"; a continuation of the exploits of Junior (Michael Oliver), an adopted orphan boy who deliberately wreaks comedic havoc everywhere he goes.
John Ritter returns as Junior's adopted father, Ben Healy.
Amy Yasbeck, who played Ben's wife, Flo, in the first movie, also returns, as school nurse Annie Young.
It was produced by producer Robert Simonds, who also produced the first one.
It was rated PG-13, unlike its predecessor, which was rated PG.
Brian Stephen Howser (February 8, 1975 – August 18, 1998) was an American professional wrestler best known for wrestling in Maryland Championship Wrestling.
In the promotion, he was the first and only wrestler to win the Light Heavyweight Championship and was nicknamed "The Lifetime MCW Light Heavyweight Champion" in tribute to his death.
Each year, the Shane Shamrock Memorial Cup is held as a tribute to him.
Despite sharing an in-ring last name, he is not related to Ken Shamrock (who was born Kenneth Wayne Kilpatrick, although he adopted the surname "Shamrock" after his adopted father, Bob Shamrock).

Father Thadeus Nguyễn Văn Lý (born 15 May 1946) is a Vietnamese Roman Catholic priest and dissident involved in many pro-democracy movements, for which he was imprisoned for a total of almost 15 years.
For his ongoing imprisonment and continuous non-violent protest, Amnesty International adopted Father Lý in December 1983 as a prisoner of conscience.
Most recently, his support for the Bloc 8406 manifesto has led to his sentence on 30 March 2007, for an additional eight years in prison, where he was released and then returned in 2011.
Philippus the son of Philip V, half-brother of Perseus Antigonid King of Macedon.
He was adopted by his brother as his son.
Even after the birth of the king's son, Alexander, Perseus treated him as his heir to the throne.
Together they surrendered to the Romans ending the Third Macedonian War.
He was led in triumph before the car of Lucius Aemilius Paullus and afterwards consigned to captivity at Alba Fucens, where he survived his adopted father but a short time.
He was only eighteen years old at the time of his death.
Sumako Matsui (松井 須磨子 , Matsui Sumako , November 1, 1886 – January 5, 1919) was a Japanese actress and singer.

Born as Masako Kobayashi in Matsushiro, Nagano, Nagano Prefecture as the fifth daughter and last of nine children of Tohta Kobayashi, she was adopted by the Hasegawa family in Ueda at the age of six and in 1900 graduated Ueda school.
She had to return to her birth family after her adopted father died, however in the year of her return, her natural father also died.
At the age of 17 she moved to Tokyo.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What was Robert Tree Cody's adopted father's most prominent PSA role?
hop1_docs: Robert Tree Cody | Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.  He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.  Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Buffalo Bill Boyhood Home | The Buffalo Bill Boyhood Home was built by Isaac Cody, the father of Buffalo Bill Cody in 1841 at LeClaire, Iowa.  The house was purchased as a tourist attraction by the Chicago, Burlington and Quincy Railroad and was moved to Cody, Wyoming, Buffalo Bill's adopted hometown, in 1933.

Cody Estes | Cody Estes (born March 13, 1994) is an American actor.  His most recent role was as Charlie Cohen in "Crossing Jordan", but his most frequent role has been younger versions of John "J.D." Dorian on the medical comedy "Scrubs".

LGBT adoption and parenting in Australia | Family law in Australia with regard to children is often based on what is considered to be in the best interest of the child.  The traditional and often used assumption is that children need both a mother and a father, which plays an important role in divorce and custodial proceedings, and has carried over into adoption and fertility procedures.  As of February 2017 all Australian states and territories except for the Northern Territory allow adoption by same-sex couples.

The Father Clements Story | The Father Clements Story is a 1987 made-for-television movie about the life of Father George Clements, an African-American Roman Catholic priest who became famous for being the first United States priest to legally adopt a child.  The movie starred actors Lou Gossett, Jr., Malcolm-Jamal Warner, and Carroll O'Connor.  Gossett, Jr. played Clements, Warner played Clements' adopted son, and O'Connor played Cardinal John Cody, the Archbishop of Chicago.  The movie was directed by Edwin Sherin.  The film score was composed by Mark Snow.

Murder of Robert Schwartz | The murder of Robert Schwartz occurred on December 8, 2001 in Leesburg, Virginia.  The crime was orchestrated by his 20-year-old daughter, Clara Jane Schwartz, as part of a fantasy role-playing game.  Clara was convicted of first-degree murder for orchestrating her father's murder.  The case made national headlines due to Robert Schwartz's prominence in the scientific community and for claims that his murder was related to role-playing games and the occult.

Reed A. Albee | Reed Adalbert Albee (8 September 1885 – 2 August 1961) was an American businessman.  He is most noted as the adoptive father of the American playwright Edward Albee and for being a member of a prominent East Coast family who owned several theaters.
hop1_titles: LGBT adoption and parenting in Australia
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states: "Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody" (Robert Tree Cody was born April 20, 1951). Bridge entity: Iron Eyes Cody. Missing fact: which public-service announcement (PSA)/advertising role Iron Eyes Cody was most prominently known for. Retrieval direction/evidence anchors: look up Iron Eyes Cody's biography or filmography and sources about PSAs/advertising—search for "Iron Eyes Cody PSA", "Keep America Beautiful", or "Crying Indian" to locate the specific PSA role.
response.hop2_query: Iron Eyes Cody Crying Indian PSA Keep America Beautiful
hop2_docs: Iron Eyes Cody | Iron Eyes Cody (born Espera Oscar de Corti April 3, 1904 – January 4, 1999) was an Italian-American actor.  He portrayed Native Americans in Hollywood films.  He also played a Native American shedding a tear about litter in one of the country's most well-known television PSAs, "Keep America Beautiful".  In 1996, Cody's half-sister said that he was of Italian ancestry, but he denied it.

Robert Tree Cody | Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.  He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.  Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Cody complex | The Cody complex is a Paleo-Indian culture group first identified at a bison antiquus kill site near Cody, Wyoming in 1951.  Points possessing characteristics of Cody Complex flaking have been found all across North America from Canada to as far south as Oklahoma and Texas.

Keep America Beautiful | Keep America Beautiful is a Stamford, CT based non profit organization founded in 1953.  It is the largest community improvement organization in the United States, with more than 620 state and community-based affiliate organizations (similar to local chapters) and more than 1,000 community organizations that participate in their programs.

Grayeagle | Grayeagle is a 1977 Western adventure film directed Charles B. Pierce, written by Brad White, Charles B. Pierce and Michael O. Sajbel, starring Ben Johnson, Iron Eyes Cody, and Lana Wood.  The music was composed by Jaime Mendoza-Nava.  The theme is about kidnapping and interracial/cross-cultural romance.

Perils of the Royal Mounted | Perils of the Royal Mounted (1942) was the 18th serial released by Columbia Pictures.  It starred Robert Kellard as the hero, Sgt. Mack MacLane of the Royal Mounties, and Kenneth MacDonald as Mort Ramsome, the head villain.  It also co-starred Nell O'Day, Iron Eyes Cody, Kermit Maynard and I. Stanford Jolley.

Chase Iron Eyes | Chase Iron Eyes is an American Indian activist, attorney, politician, and a member of the Standing Rock Sioux Tribe.  He is a member of the Lakota People's Law Project and a co-founder of the Native American news website "Last Real Indians".  In April 2016 he announced his candidacy for the United States House of Representatives for North Dakota's at-large congressional district.  He was unsuccessful.
response.summary_2: Robert Tree Cody's adopted father was actor Iron Eyes Cody. Iron Eyes Cody was best known for appearing as the "Crying Indian" in the Keep America Beautiful PSA — a Native American shedding a tear over litter (passage [1]).
response.answer: the "Crying Indian" in the Keep America Beautiful PSA
